chunk_summary: This section discusses integrating clinic care with school health platforms in India to improve adolescent nutrition outcomes, focusing on anemia, obesity, and athlete-specific risks using national programs like Ayushman Bharat, PM-POSHAN, and Anemia Mukt Bharat.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of national programs like Ayushman Bharat and PM-POSHAN in adolescent nutrition care.
Learn strategies to transition clinical care to school-based platforms for anemia, obesity, and athlete-specific risks.
Identify key components of the Anemia Mukt Bharat approach, including screening, treatment, and follow-up.
Explore practical dietary guidance for adolescents in school meal programs.
key_facts: The Anemia Mukt Bharat approach includes screening, treating, testing for response, and addressing causes of anemia.
School-based Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) is recommended after clinical treatment for anemia.
Deworming should be coordinated with the National Deworming Day (NDD) schedule.
The PM-POSHAN program supports practical dietary guidance, emphasizing pulses, eggs, and milk while avoiding sugar-sweetened beverages.
For athletes, a food-first plan and avoidance of unregulated supplements are advised, with a focus on RED-S risk markers.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Operational guidelines on School Health Programme under Ayushman Bharat — https://nhm.gov.in/New_Updates_2018/NHM_Components/RMNCHA/AH/guidelines/Ope
document_key_topics: Integration of clinic care with school health platforms
Anemia management and prevention in adolescents
Obesity and lifestyle risk management in schools
Nutritional guidance for athletes and supplement safety
